ucs.title: [LLM] Patchwork (Dropping Elephant) Equation Editor exploit chain — EQNEDT32.exe spawns scripting / LOLBin child
ucs.description: Hunts Patchwork's signature delivery chain: a weaponised RTF/DOC lure exploiting CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2018-0802 in Microsoft Equation Editor (EQNEDT32.exe), which then spawns a scripting host (mshta/wscript/powershell/cmd) or LOLBin (mshta/regsvr32/certutil) to stage BADNEWS, NDiskMonitor or QuasarRAT. This is the most consistently observed initial-access pattern in Patchwork campaigns (Operation Hangover, MONSOON, BADNEWS) since 2017 and is essentially never seen as benign behaviour.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="EQNEDT32.EXE" Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe","schtasks.exe") by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.user _time span=1m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename firstTime as first_seen lastTime as last_seen | convert ctime(first_seen) ctime(last_seen) | sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
// Patchwork (G0040) — Equation Editor exploit chain (CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2018-0802)
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe","schtasks.exe","forfiles.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "eqnedt32.exe"   // Equation Editor — Patchwork's preferred CVE-2017-11882 trigger
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (_suspicious_children)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-EncodedCommand","DownloadString","AppData\\Local\\Temp","\\ProgramData\\","-w hidden","/c start")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentSHA256= InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Patchwork (G0040) — Equation Editor exploit chain on Sentinel SecurityEvent (4688)
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe","schtasks.exe","forfiles.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\EQNEDT32.EXE"          // Patchwork CVE-2017-11882 trigger
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in (_suspicious_children)
   or CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-EncodedCommand","DownloadString","AppData\\Local\\Temp","\\ProgramData\\","-w hidden","/c start")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Patchwork APT - Equation Editor Spawning Scripting/LOLBin Child
id: 8b1a4e1d-1f4a-4f2c-9c0a-7e9bf2c2a401
status: experimental
description: Detects CVE-2017-11882/CVE-2018-0802 exploitation chain associated with Patchwork (Dropping Elephant, MONSOON, G0040). Equation Editor (EQNEDT32.EXE) should never legitimately launch a scripting host or LOLBin.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0040/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.g0040
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\EQNEDT32.EXE'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\hh.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - None expected; Equation Editor has been deprecated and should not spawn child processes.
level: high
ucs.rationale: Patchwork has been documented by Forcepoint (MONSOON), Cymmetria (Operation BugDrop / Hangover), Trend Micro and Volexity using weaponised RTFs/DOCs that trigger CVE-2017-11882 in Equation Editor as the first-stage code-execution primitive across nearly every campaign from 2017 onward. EQNEDT32.exe spawning ANY child process is anomalous (the editor doesn't legitimately do this), and pairing the p

ucs.title: [LLM] Patchwork BADNEWS dead-drop resolver beacon — non-browser HTTP fetch from blogspot/forum/pastebin
ucs.description: Hunts the BADNEWS / NDiskMonitor C2 retrieval pattern that has defined Patchwork tradecraft for nearly a decade: the implant calls out to a hard-coded set of legitimate web services (Blogspot, WordPress, Forum posts, GitHub raw, Pastebin, feedburner) to fetch an encoded blob containing the real C2 address (T1102.001 dead-drop resolver). The detection finds non-browser, non-Office processes (LOLBins, rundll32, schtasks-spawned binaries, AppData-resident binaries) reaching these specific resolver hosts.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Web.url) as urls values(Web.dest) as dest values(Web.user) as user min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Web where (Web.url="*blogspot.*" OR Web.url="*wordpress.com*" OR Web.url="*pastebin.com*" OR Web.url="*paste.ee*" OR Web.url="*feedburner.com*" OR Web.url="*raw.githubusercontent.com*" OR Web.url="*forum*") Web.app NOT IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","safari.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","slack.exe") by host Web.app Web.user Web.url _time span=5m | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)` | rename firstTime as first_seen lastTime as last_seen | convert ctime(first_seen) ctime(last_seen) | where count >= 2 | sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
// Patchwork (G0040) — BADNEWS/NDiskMonitor dead-drop resolver beacon (T1102.001)
let _resolver_hosts = dynamic(["blogspot.","blogger.com","wordpress.com","pastebin.com","paste.ee","feedburner.com","raw.githubusercontent.com","gist.githubusercontent.com","sites.google.com"]);
let _browser_procs = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","slack.exe","acrord32.exe"]);
let _suspicious_user_paths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Roaming\",@"\AppData\Local\Temp\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\Public\",@"\Users\Public\"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_browser_procs)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (_resolver_hosts)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe")
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_suspicious_user_paths)
| summarize HitCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            UrlSamples = make_set(RemoteUrl, 10),
            CmdSamples = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 5)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Patchwork (G0040) — dead-drop resolver beacon, ASIM cross-vendor
let _resolver_hosts = dynamic(["blogspot.","blogger.com","wordpress.com","pastebin.com","paste.ee","feedburner.com","raw.githubusercontent.com","gist.githubusercontent.com","sites.google.com"]);
let _browser_procs = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","slack.exe","acrord32.exe"]);
let _suspicious_user_paths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Roaming\",@"\AppData\Local\Temp\",@"\ProgramData\",@"\Public\"]);
ImWebSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where isnotempty(Url)
| where Url has_any (_resolver_hosts)
| extend ProcLower = tolower(ActingProcessName)
| where isnotempty(ProcLower)
| where ProcLower !in~ (_browser_procs)
| where SrcUsername !endswith "$"
| where ProcLower in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe")
   or ActingProcessCommandLine has_any (_suspicious_user_paths)
| summarize HitCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            UrlSamples = make_set(Url, 10),
            CmdSamples = make_set(ActingProcessCommandLine, 5)
            by DvcHostname, SrcUsername, ActingProcessName
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Patchwork's BADNEWS family (documented by Cymmetria 'Unfinished Business', Forcepoint MONSOON, Volexity, Trend Micro) is famous for hiding the real C2 address inside a comment / encoded blob on Blogspot, WordPress, GitHub gists and forum posts — a deliberate dead-drop resolver pattern (T1102.001). Patchwork's tooling characteristically drops the implant under %APPDATA% or %ProgramData% and beacons

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActorUsername` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImAuthentication, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
